title: Selectable columns in the preview pane when accessing an item from a dashboard- select what is seen
excerpt:
Hi, we have a board with many columns that shows all sort of differing information.

posts.author: Paul_Adams (Paul Adams)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, we have a board with many columns that shows all sort of differing information.
This board is mainly used by a team of designers, we use a dashboard view to schedule work through a timeline. When we click the timeline we can access ALL columns. The user then has to scroll through that to input information. What id like to be able to do is select what columns are visible in the preview pane to make it easier for them to complete relevant tasks.

posts.author: michelle653burkes (michelle)
posts.cookedHtml:
Paul Adams:

Hi, we have a board with many columns that shows all sort of differing information.
This board is mainly used by a team of designers, we use a dashboard view to schedule work through a timeline. When we click the timeline we can access ALL columns. The user then has to scroll through that to input information. What id like to be able to do is select what columns are visible in the preview pane to make it easier for them to complete relevant tasks

Hello!
To streamline your team’s workflow, you can customize the board view in your project management tool to select which columns are visible in the preview pane. Look for a settings option that controls column visibility or create custom views that include only the relevant columns for the timeline view. Additionally, using templates with pre-configured columns can ensure consistency and speed up the information input process. This approach should make it easier for your designers to complete relevant tasks without navigating through unnecessary columns.
